This feature allows you to display a por: This feature allows you to display a portion of the nearby {{sections}}. This can be useful to give a hint to the user that there is more content to see. You can adjust the amount of the nearby {{sections}} that will be visible.

You can use this option to reserve space: You can use this option to reserve space for other contents like the site menu to appear beside the slider.
Specifies the minimum space on left and: Specifies the minimum space on left and right sides of content wrapper. This space appears when the screen size becomes smaller that the content wrapper width.
Enable this feature to load your {{docum: Enable this feature to load your {{document}} only when they are about to appear on the screen. This will make your page load faster and improve the user experience.

Enable this feature to navigate between: Enable this feature to navigate between {{sections}} using the browser’s native scroll behavior. This enables a smoother and more natural navigation yet exciting experience for your users.
Enable native scroll navigation. This op: Enable native scroll navigation. This option allows you to navigate between {{sections}} using the browser’s native scroll behavior. This options is available only when the layout is set to “Full Screen”.
Elevate your user experience with auto s: Elevate your user experience with auto scroll navigation!
Enable this feature to automatically scr: Enable this feature to automatically scroll between {{sections}}. This feature provides a smooth and seamless navigation experience for your users.

This is used to identify the input eleme: This is used to identify the input element in the contacts list. It should be unique within the form and should not contain any spaces and special characters.
The value of the autocomplete attribute,: The value of the autocomplete attribute, which can be used to help users fill in forms more quickly.
The unique identifier for this hidden fi: The unique identifier for this hidden field. This will be the name attribute of the input element and the key used when the form is submitted.
Choose how the hidden field gets its val: Choose how the hidden field gets its value. The field can use a static value or dynamically retrieve data from various sources.
Use a fixed, predefined value that never:
Use a fixed, predefined value that never changes.

Best for: Product IDs, configuration values, or any constant data.

Example: "product_123" or "vip_customer"
Extract value from the current page URL:
Extract value from the current page URL parameters.

Best for: Referral codes, campaign tracking, or user-specific data passed via URL.

Example: ?ref=partner123 → reads "partner123"
Retrieve value from browser's local stor:
Retrieve value from browser's local storage (persists across sessions).

Best for: User preferences, saved settings, or long-term data storage.

Example: user_preferences, theme_settings
Retrieve value from browser's session st:
Retrieve value from browser's session storage (cleared when tab closes).

Best for: Temporary data, cart tokens, or session-specific information.

Example: cart_token, temp_user_id
Read value from a browser cookie.\n\nBes:
Read value from a browser cookie.

Best for: Tracking IDs, authentication tokens, or data that needs to persist across browser sessions.

Example: tracking_id, auth_token
Access a variable from the global JavaSc:
Access a variable from the global JavaScript scope (without window prefix).

Best for: Configuration objects, app state, or variables set by other scripts.

Example: CONFIG.apiKey, APP.user.authToken, SITE_SETTINGS.theme
Enter the exact value that will be submi: Enter the exact value that will be submitted with the form. This value will be constant and never change.
The name of the URL parameter to extract: The name of the URL parameter to extract the value from. For example, if your URL is "?ref=partner123", enter "ref" to get "partner123".
The key used to store the value in local: The key used to store the value in localStorage. This data persists across browser sessions until manually cleared.
The key used to store the value in sessi: The key used to store the value in sessionStorage. This data is cleared when the browser tab is closed.
The name of the cookie to read the value: The name of the cookie to read the value from. Cookies persist across browser sessions and can be set by your server or client-side code.
The path to the global variable (without: The path to the global variable (without "window." prefix). Use dot notation for nested properties. For example: "CONFIG.apiKey" or "APP.user.authToken".
